request.question: Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.

hypothesis.intervention: conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles
hypothesis.threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: doxorubicin folate PLGA nanoparticles HeLa intracellular accumulation
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Conjugating doxorubicin to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles will increase intracellular drug accumulation in HeLa cells by at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin, due to folate receptor-mediated endocytosis overexpressed in cancer cells, while reducing cytotoxicity in folate receptor-negative HEK293 cells by at least 40%.

The papers focus on general drug delivery systems or PLGA as a carrier but do not cover the specific combination or biological system outlined in the hypothesis.

plan.objective: To evaluate the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment will involve the synthesis of folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles loaded with doxorubicin. HeLa cells will be treated with both the conjugated nanoparticles and free doxorubicin. The intracellular drug accumulation will be quantified using fluorescence spectroscopy, comparing the two treatment groups.
controls: Free doxorubicin at a concentration of 10 µM.
Untreated HeLa cells without any drug treatment.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Dissolve 100 mg of PLGA (Resomer RG 503H, Evonik) in 5 mL of dichloromethane (DCM) at room temperature. Add 10 mg of doxorubicin (doxorubicin hydrochloride, Sigma-Aldrich) to the PLGA solution and stir for 30 min to ensure complete dissolution.
Equipment: Magnetic stirrer, Beaker (50 mL)
Reagents: 100 mg PLGA in 5 mL DCM, 10 mg doxorubicin
Expected outcome: Doxorubicin is fully dissolved in the PLGA solution.
Safety: DCM is a volatile organic solvent; work in a fume hood.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Add 5 mL of 1% w/v polyvinyl alcohol (PVA, MW 30,000-70,000, Sigma-Aldrich) aqueous solution to the PLGA-doxorubicin solution. Emulsify using a probe sonicator (Sonics & Materials, VCX 130) at 30% amplitude for 2 min to form an emulsion.
Equipment: Probe sonicator, Beaker (50 mL)
Reagents: 5 mL 1% w/v PVA solution
Expected outcome: Stable emulsion is formed with no visible phase separation.
Safety: Use appropriate ear protection when operating the sonicator.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Nanoparticle Synthesis
Transfer the emulsion to a 50 mL centrifuge tube and add 20 mL of distilled water. Stir for 1 h at room temperature to allow solvent evaporation and nanoparticle formation.
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5810R), 50 mL centrifuge tube
Expected outcome: Formation of PLGA nanoparticles with encapsulated doxorubicin.

DAY 1 | 40 min | Nanoparticle Purification
Centrifuge the nanoparticle suspension at 10,000 × g for 30 min at 4°C. Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in 5 mL of distilled water.
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5810R), 50 mL centrifuge tube
Expected outcome: Pellet of purified nanoparticles is obtained.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Folate Functionalization
Dissolve 5 mg of folic acid (Sigma-Aldrich) in 1 mL of dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) and add to the nanoparticle suspension. Stir for 2 h at room temperature to allow conjugation.
Equipment: Magnetic stirrer, Beaker (50 mL)
Reagents: 5 mg folic acid in 1 mL DMSO
Expected outcome: Folate is conjugated to the surface of the nanoparticles.
Safety: DMSO is a skin irritant; wear gloves.

DAY 1 | 48 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in 10 mL of DMEM (Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium, Sigma-Aldrich) supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS, Sigma-Aldrich) and 1% penicillin-streptomycin (Gibco) at 37°C in a 5% CO2 incubator until 80% confluence is reached (approximately 48 h).
Equipment: CO2 incubator (Thermo Scientific), T75 cell culture flask
Reagents: 10 mL DMEM, 1 mL FBS, 100 µL penicillin-streptomycin
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80% confluence in culture.

DAY 2 | 4 h | Drug Treatment
Treat HeLa cells with 1 mL of either folate-functionalized PLGA-doxorubicin nanoparticles or free doxorubicin (10 µM) for 4 h at 37°C. Include untreated cells as a control.
Equipment: CO2 incubator (Thermo Scientific), 96-well plate
Reagents: 1 mL folate-functionalized PLGA-doxorubicin nanoparticles, 1 mL free doxorubicin (10 µM)
Expected outcome: Cells are treated with both formulations for the specified duration.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Intracellular Drug Accumulation Measurement
Wash cells with PBS (phosphate-buffered saline, Sigma-Aldrich) and lyse with 100 µL of lysis buffer (1% Triton X-100 in PBS) for 30 min at room temperature. Measure fluorescence intensity at 480 nm excitation and 590 nm emission using a microplate reader (BioTek Synergy H1).
Equipment: Microplate reader (BioTek Synergy H1), 96-well plate
Reagents: 100 µL lysis buffer (1% Triton X-100 in PBS)
Expected outcome: Fluorescence intensity is measured, indicating intracellular drug accumulation.

assumptions: Folate receptors are overexpressed in HeLa cells, facilitating uptake of folate-conjugated nanoparticles.
Doxorubicin retains its cytotoxic activity when conjugated to PLGA nanoparticles.
The fluorescence intensity correlates with the amount of doxorubicin accumulated intracellularly.
decision_criteria: Proceed if intracellular doxorubicin accumulation in folate-functionalized nanoparticles is at least 3-fold higher than free doxorubicin.
Confirm successful nanoparticle synthesis through dynamic light scattering (DLS) showing particle size <200 nm.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot of PLGA and doxorubicin for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (between 10-15) for HeLa cells.
Calibrate the microplate reader before use with a standard fluorescence solution.

phases.phase_name: Day 5: Data Analysis and Results Write-up

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 3-fold compared to free doxorubicin improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To evaluate the intracellular accumulation of doxorubicin when conjugated to folate-functionalized PLGA nanoparticles in HeLa cells compared to free doxorubicin.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
